key_insights.insight: Sexual shame can stem from childhood experiences and influence how one perceives and experiences sex later in life.
key_insights.quote: I realized just how much sexual shame I was holding, so much of it. And I initially sort of wanted to fix it, wanted to do something about it... But I quickly realized that I actually had to trace it back to see where it even comes from.

key_insights.insight: Many people learn about sex from porn, which can lead to unrealistic expectations and a focus on performance rather than pleasure.
key_insights.quote: A lot of us learn that we should perform, that sex should be driven by orgasm and ejaculation and this sort of production, if you will, which is not actually accurate for most people when it comes to what really actually feels pleasurable, especially for women.

key_insights.insight: It's important to recognize when you are replicating pornographic behavior in intimate situations, even unintentionally.
key_insights.quote: I realized that even some of the positions I would get in were very much like porn because certain positions in porn are like that because the camera is there, not because it's comfortable, because that's the shot for the viewer to be able to see it.

key_insights.insight: Sobriety can reveal underlying issues, such as sexual shame, that were previously masked by alcohol or drugs.
key_insights.quote: But then when I got sober, all of those things came to the surface and then I had to look that in the eye.

key_insights.insight: Sex should be viewed as a language, where partners may have different needs, desires, and ways of expressing themselves.
key_insights.quote: I feel like I'm surrounded by men that need to start seeing sex as a language because then you can ask yourself, well, actually she's speaking Spanish and I'm speaking English.

key_insights.insight: Tantric sex can provide an alternative to performance-based sex by emphasizing connection, pleasure, and a focus on the present moment.
key_insights.quote: Tantric sex was the first thing that I came across that made me realize and really articulated that sex is actually not a specific destination.

key_insights.insight: Asking a partner directly how they like to give and receive love, can significantly improve intimacy and understanding in the relationship.
key_insights.quote: It's been a game changer to just ask the person that I'm dating or my current partner to be like how do you like to be loved how do you like to receive love and how do you like to give it.

key_insights.insight: Successful relationships require mutual development and a willingness to build satisfaction together instead of solely searching for a 'perfect fit'.
key_insights.quote: This idea that you have to actually build and develop a relationship towards a place of satisfaction as opposed to finding your perfect soulmate or perfect fit.

key_insights.insight: Respectful communication is essential, even when partners have fundamental disagreements.
key_insights.quote: The reason why it works is because of one very simple thing communication and a very healthy high respect communication where everything isn't an attempt to win it's an attempt to like genuinely understand to move forward.

actionable_advice.advice: Ask your partner about their preferred 'love languages' and sexual preferences.
actionable_advice.how_to: Initiate a direct conversation about how they like to give and receive love, and what they like/dislike sexually.

actionable_advice.advice: Explore tantric sex practices with your partner.
actionable_advice.how_to: Research tantric sex techniques and incorporate elements such as slow movements, extended foreplay, and focus on sensory experience into your intimate moments.

actionable_advice.advice: Communicate honestly about discomfort or dissatisfaction during sex.
actionable_advice.how_to: Create a safe space where you and your partner can openly express what feels good and what doesn't, even if it challenges expectations.

actionable_advice.advice: Question assumptions about what sex 'should' be like.
actionable_advice.how_to: Reflect on how porn and societal expectations have shaped your view of sex, and challenge those beliefs by prioritizing pleasure and connection.

memorable_stories: The guest discusses their journey overcoming sexual shame after becoming sober.
The host shares an anecdote about his initial reaction to his partner saying she didn't like sex, and how open communication eventually led to a more fulfilling sexual relationship.
The host shared the background of his partner, which revealed a history of toxic sexual relationship dynamics.
contrarian_claims: Finding a 'perfect fit' is less important than building a relationship through mutual development and communication.
Partners do not need to share fundamental beliefs to have a lasting, successful relationship.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 79 - The Surprising Truth About Sex: Africa Brooke
